name: WooCommerce
description: Open-source eCommerce plugin for WordPress providing REST APIs for store and admin (products, orders, customers, etc.).

auth_methods.note: Official WooCommerce REST API docs state WooCommerce uses API keys (Consumer Key/Consumer Secret). Over HTTPS it supports HTTP Basic Authentication using the consumer key as username and consumer secret as password; without SSL it supports OAuth 1.0a.

credential_access.note: Official docs describe creating REST API keys in the WordPress admin under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API by selecting Add key for a user, generating a consumer key and consumer secret (no vendor approval flow indicated).

api_surface.note: Official REST API v3 covers most store objects (products, orders, customers, coupons, settings, etc.). There is also a separate Store API for storefront/customer interactions (see store API docs).

buildability.note: WooCommerce's official REST API documentation describes supported authentication (API keys with Basic Auth over HTTPS or OAuth 1.0a) and self-serve REST API key generation in the WooCommerce admin, enabling immediate development for anyone with access to a WooCommerce site.

queries: WooCommerce REST API authentication consumer key consumer secret generate API keys WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API
WooCommerce REST API requires paid plan
